heroHeadline: GSF Pledges Exceed 5,500-Member Target; 1,343 Gang Members Killed Since December; Qatar Pledges $30M
heroSubtitle: The Gang Suppression Force recorded a major milestone on April 23, 2026, as Special Representative Jack Christofides told the UN Security Council that national pledges to the force now exceed its authorized 5,500-member ceiling — with more than $200 million pledged by 13 states ($59 million disbursed) and Qatar confirming a $30 million contribution. Christofides disclosed for the first time that GSF operations between December 2025 and February 2026 resulted in 1,343 suspected gang members killed. The GSF's current advance force of approximately 545 personnel (400 Chadian, 70 Salvadoran, 75 Guatemalan) continues joint operations with the Haitian National Police while the full 5,500-person force scales toward fall–end 2026 deployment. On April 24, official reporting confirmed the mission remains in an 'early phase of establishment' with its operational framework — covering joint operations, detentions, and HNP handoffs — now finalized. The Kenya-led MSS mission concluded its phased drawdown on April 21 with 803 total officers withdrawn. Despite the funding breakthrough, 23 communes remain inaccessible to state authority, the August 30 election date is in serious jeopardy following the April 8 indefinite postponement of voter registration, 6.4 million Haitians need humanitarian assistance, and the 2026 Humanitarian Response Plan remains only ~20% funded ($172M of $880M). The GSF also faces the challenge that gang clashes in the Plaine du Cul-de-Sac (April 20) sent stray gunfire to Toussaint Louverture Airport, underscoring how fragile the security environment remains for the advance force.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN BINUH, OHCHR, OCHA, IOM, Reuters, AP, ABC News, Miami Herald, Al Jazeera, France 24, Le Nouvelliste, ACLED, ICG, HRW, WFP, Haitian Times, Jamaica Observer, Jamaica Gleaner, RNDDH, PassBlue, Amnesty International, IMF, HaitiLibre, ICI Haiti, JURIST, International Rescue Committee, NPR, NBC News, Capital FM Kenya, Dominican Today, Security Council Report, UN News, Xinhua, Haiti Sun
